content: For creative-production domains with deep worldbuilding, splitting artifacts into subcategory files (world-bible/geography.json, world-bible/magic-system.json, world-bible/politics.json, world-bible/culture.json, world-bible/history.json) rather than monolithic files is critical. This enables targeted loading — a chapter-drafter referencing magic rules doesn't ingest geography data. The run produced 42 artifacts across 18 schema files, with per-chapter artifacts (chapters/{N}/draft.md, chapters/{N}/revised.md, chapters/{N}/final.md) and per-lens beta feedback (beta-feedback/{N}/romance-lens.json, fantasy-lens.json, etc.). This granularity aligned naturally with the sub-coordinator structure.
sourceEvidence: architecture.json domainSpecific artifacts: world-bible/geography.json, world-bible/magic-system.json, world-bible/politics.json, world-bible/culture.json, world-bible/history.json (each with single specialist writer). chapters/{N}/draft.md, beta-feedback/{N}/romance-lens.json, etc. Domain-brief I28: 'content MUST be decomposed into subcategory files within typed directories — not stored as monolithic files.'

history.reason: Cold start — first observation from initial factory run
